title: Makeup on Cotton
stainChemistry: Makeup on cotton covers a family of related stains: foundation, concealer, BB cream, cushion compact, bronzer, blush. They share the same structure — oil or silicone vehicle (dimethicone, cyclomethicone, mineral oil, or plant oils) + pigment particles (iron oxide, titanium dioxide, carmine, mica) + waxes + emulsifiers. The oil/silicone vehicle is what locks the pigment against the fiber; remove the vehicle and the pigment lifts. Lipstick and mascara have their own cards with dye / wax emphasis; this card is the foundation/concealer family. Cotton tolerates IPA, POG, NSD, 3% H₂O₂, and hot wash.
whyThisWorks: Same solvent-first pattern as lipstick and ink. Silicone-based foundations (most modern ones) are especially water-repellent — soap alone spreads them without removing. IPA or micellar water breaks up the silicone + oil vehicle; the pigment transfers onto a catch cloth; NSD emulsifies the residual film; peroxide clears any dye shadow on whites.
defaultAssumption: Fresh makeup on a collar or cuff is a high-confidence DIY. Long-wear / matte foundations have more polymer than traditional cream and need 2–3 solvent passes. Dark shades leave more pigment residue than nude shades.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Dull blade, clean white cotton towel catch cloth
spottingProtocol.instruction: Gently scrape any cakey residue off with a dull blade — don't rub. Place a folded clean white catch cloth UNDER the stain so pigment transfers downward when solvent mobilizes it.

spottingProtocol.technique: Solvent on cloth, tamp from back
spottingProtocol.equipment: Isopropyl alcohol 90%+ OR POG on clean white cloth (micellar water for silicone-based)
spottingProtocol.instruction: IPA is the pro default. For silicone-based foundations (most modern long-wear), micellar water or POG works better than alcohol. Apply solvent to a white cloth, tamp from the BACK. Flip cloth frequently. 2–3 passes usually clear fresh makeup.
Apply NSD, work with a soft brush. Emulsifies the residual oil/silicone film. Rinse cool from the back.
For residual pigment shadow on whites: 3% H₂O₂ drop-by-drop. Oxidizes the pigment color (especially iron oxide tints). Skip on dark or dyed cotton.
Launder hot per care label. Inspect BEFORE the dryer.

steps: 1. Scrape bulk. Catch cloth UNDER the stain.
2. IPA or POG on a white cloth, tamp from back. Micellar water for silicone-based foundations. 2–3 passes.
3. NSD — emulsify residual oil/silicone. Cool rinse.
4. 3% H₂O₂ on pigment shadow (whites only). 3–5 min.
professionalProtocol.temperature: cool through spotting; hot wash post-chemistry
warnings: Solvent before water — water alone doesn't touch makeup
Catch cloth UNDER + pressing cloth on TOP — prevents sideways pigment spread
Silicone-based long-wear needs micellar or POG; IPA alone may plateau
One oxidizer at a time; skip peroxide on dark/dyed cotton

homeSolutions.instruction: If any solid makeup residue is sitting on the surface (cushion compact, pressed powder), lift it off with a dull butter knife or credit-card edge. Don't rub — rubbing grinds the pigment into the weave.

homeSolutions.agent: Put a White Towel UNDER the Stain
homeSolutions.instruction: Fold a clean white towel and place it under the stained area. When the next step dissolves the makeup, the pigment transfers DOWN into the catch cloth instead of smearing sideways into clean fabric.

For long-wear / silicone-based foundations (most modern brands — Estée Lauder Double Wear, MAC Studio Fix, Fenty Pro Filt'r): MICELLAR WATER is the unlock. Dab a cotton round or clean cloth with micellar water and press onto the stain from the back. The micelles specifically target silicone and oil vehicles. For traditional cream foundations or powder: 70%+ rubbing alcohol on a white cloth works just as well. Flip the pressing cloth to a clean spot every few seconds — pigment transfers out. Expect 2–3 rounds.

homeSolutions.agent: Dawn + Soft Brush for the Oil
homeSolutions.instruction: Work a drop of Dawn dish soap into the spot with a soft toothbrush. Dawn breaks up any residual oil film. Rinse cool.
For a residual tint shadow on white cotton: dab 3% drugstore hydrogen peroxide, let it bubble 3–5 min, blot. Skip on dark or dyed cotton.
homeSolutions.safetyNote: Never mix peroxide with chlorine bleach or ammonia — toxic fume risk.

homeSolutions.agent: Hot Wash, Inspect Before the Dryer
homeSolutions.instruction: Launder on the hottest setting the care label allows. Inspect BEFORE the dryer. Repeat the solvent + Dawn round if color remains.

If the makeup has been through a hot dryer, the oil and pigment have set. Take to a dry cleaner. Tell them: "Makeup on cotton — [foundation / concealer / blush / bronzer], age [fresh / overnight / heat-set], home-treated with [micellar water / rubbing alcohol] + Dawn + 3% peroxide."

steps: 3. Micellar water (silicone-based) OR rubbing alcohol (cream/powder) on a white cloth, tamp from the BACK. 2–3 passes.
4. Dawn + soft toothbrush for residual oil.
5. 3% hydrogen peroxide on residual tint (whites only).
6. Hot wash per care label. Inspect BEFORE the dryer.
products: Micellar water (Bioderma, Simple, Garnier — the generic bottle on any drugstore shelf)
70%+ rubbing alcohol (alternative for cream / powder formulas)
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop if makeup has been hot-dried and two home rounds haven't cleared it, or if the cotton is dark dyed and peroxide isn't safe.
warnings: Solvent before water — water spreads oil-based makeup
Silicone-based (long-wear) needs micellar or POG, not just alcohol
Never mix peroxide with chlorine bleach or ammonia

neverDo: Never rub wet makeup into cotton — smears pigment into clean fabric
Never pour solvent on cotton — tamp with cloth
Never skip the catch cloth — sideways pigment spread
Never hot-dry residual makeup — sets pigment permanently
fiberSensitivities: Cotton tolerates IPA, POG, micellar water, NSD, 3% H₂O₂, hot wash
Dyed cotton: skip peroxide or inside-seam-test
Heat-durable once the makeup is out

materialWarnings: Solvent first — water alone doesn't touch makeup
Silicone-based formulas need micellar water or POG
Catch cloth UNDER + tamp on TOP

escalation.when: Makeup heat-set by dryer or iron, two home rounds failed, or dark dyed cotton where peroxide isn't safe.
escalation.whatToTell: "Makeup on cotton — [type if known], age [fresh / overnight / older], home-treated with micellar water + Dawn + 3% H₂O₂. [Hot-washed / not yet laundered.]"

contextQuestions: What kind of makeup — foundation, concealer, blush, bronzer, cushion compact?
Long-wear / matte / 24hr formula, or traditional cream?
Is the cotton white, light, or dark/dyed?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Silicone-based long-wear formulas are the main variable — micellar water is the specific unlock; plain alcohol may plateau. Dark shades leave more residual pigment than nudes.
